name: Kraig Biocraft Laboratories, Inc.
summary: Kraig Biocraft Laboratories, Inc. is a Wyoming-based biotechnology company that develops recombinant spider silk fibers using genetically engineered silkworms. The company inserts spider silk gene sequences into silkworm embryos to produce fibers combining spider silk and native silkworm proteins. Target applications include performance apparel, workwear, ballistic-resistant materials, medical implants, filtration, luxury fashion, and flexible composites. Production is conducted in Vietnam through its wholly owned subsidiary Prodigy Silk Co. Ltd.

products.name: Dragon Silk
products.description: Legacy recombinant spider silk fiber produced by genetically engineered silkworms for technical textile applications.

products.name: Monster Silk
products.description: Legacy recombinant spider silk fiber with unique mechanical properties developed via genetic engineering.

products.name: Recombinant Spider Silk Fiber
products.description: Next-generation spider silk fibers with increased purity and performance for apparel, medical, and industrial uses.

partnerships.description: Initial research collaboration on recombinant spider silk technology; research transitioned in-house in October 2017.
MOU and final agreements signed to enhance sericulture in Vietnam using spider silk silkworm technology.

partnerships.partner_name: Lam Dong Agro-Forestry Research & Experiment Center (LAREC)
partnerships.description: Partnership to enhance sericulture in Vietnam through expanded application of spider silk silkworm technology.
Standby Equity Purchase Agreement allowing Company to sell up to $10 million in common stock to the investor.
Provided funding support enabling the Company to transition research operations out of Notre Dame in 2017.
